note: EUR-Lex CELEX:32014L0068 resolves correctly to Directive 2014/68/EU (PED). PED Article 4 thresholds (>0.5 bar gauge, PS×V >25/50 bar·L) are correctly described. Categories I–IV per Annex II are accurate. Notified Body requirement for Cat III/IV with Module B+D is correct. EN 13445 and EN 13480 are recognised harmonised standards under PED. Gap description (no China–EU MRA for pressure equipment) is accurate. Mandatory status correctly scoped.

EUR-Lex CELEX:32006L0042 resolves correctly to Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC. Regulation (EU) 2023/1230 exists and has been confirmed on EUR-Lex; application date is 20 January 2027 (fragment says 'from 2027' — correct). EN ISO 22734 reference as harmonised product standard for hydrogen generators is correct. Self-declaration note (no mandatory NB under Machinery Directive alone, unless PED overlap) is accurate. Minor: the tag on the 2023/1230 transition date can now be resolved — date is confirmed as 20 January 2027.

EUR-Lex CELEX:32014L0034 resolves correctly to ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU. EN IEC 60079-0:2018 is confirmed as a harmonised standard under 2014/34/EU. EN IEC 60079-1 (flameproof 'd'), 60079-11 (intrinsic safety 'i'), 60079-15 (type 'n') all exist in the EN IEC 60079 series. EN 1127-1 (explosive atmospheres basic concepts) exists. Category 1G/2G NB requirement is correct. Gap (GB 3836 not accepted in EU, no MRA) is accurate. Hydrogen Group IIC, T1 classification is standard practice and accurate.

Source URL (CELEX:32006L0042) is correct for Machinery Directive. EN ISO 22734:2019 title 'Hydrogen generators using water electrolysis — Industrial, commercial and residential applications' is confirmed correct. LVD 2014/35/EU and EMC 2014/30/EU references are accurate. Machinery Regulation 2023/1230 transition date tag: confirmed 20 January 2027 — fragment's 'from 2027' is correct. EN ISO 13849-1 (safety-related parts of control systems) is a real standard. Gap analysis (no unified CN machinery-CE pathway) is accurate.

SOURCE URL ERROR (fixed): source.url was 'https://www.iso.org/standard/71940.html' which resolves to ISO 19880-1:2020 (Gaseous hydrogen fuelling stations), NOT ISO 22734. Correct URL is 'https://www.iso.org/standard/69212.html' for ISO 22734:2019. Fix has been applied to the fragment file. Standard content is otherwise correct: ISO 22734:2019 title and scope (industrial/commercial/residential water electrolysis hydrogen generators) are confirmed. Machinery Regulation 2023/1230 application date '2027-01-14' in standards_or_laws field is incorrect — confirmed date is 20 January 2027 (fragment text body says 'from 2027' which is fine, but the structured field says '2027-01-14'). Mandatory status description is accurate.

EUR-Lex CELEX:32023R1184 resolves correctly to Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/1184 of 10 February 2023 (RFNBO methodology under RED II/RED III). The regulation covers additionality, temporal and geographic correlation requirements, and the 70% GHG saving threshold — all confirmed by EUR-Lex and multiple secondary sources. Directive 2023/2413 (RED III) reference is confirmed: it amends Directive (EU) 2018/2001 and entered into force 20 November 2023. ISO 14687:2019 (Hydrogen fuel quality — Product specification) confirmed on iso.org/standard/69539.html. RFNBO as market-access criterion (not a safety mandate) correctly characterised. Note: ISO 14687 has been updated in 2025 (ISO 14687:2025) — fragment references 2019 edition which was current at authoring; consider update to 2025 edition.

EUR-Lex CELEX:32019R1020 resolves correctly to Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 on market surveillance and compliance. Article 4 responsible economic operator requirement for non-EU manufacturers confirmed. PED (2014/68/EU), ATEX (2014/34/EU), Machinery Directive (2006/42/EC), LVD (2014/35/EU), EMC (2014/30/EU) multi-directive bundle is correct for electrolyzers. Notified Body mandatory for PED Cat III–IV and ATEX Cat 1G/2G confirmed. Machinery Regulation 2023/1230 transition date tag: confirmed as 20 January 2027 — matches 'applies from 2027' in fragment. Decision 768/2008/EC (modular conformity assessment framework) is a real document. EN 13445 and EN 60079 series references are accurate. 18–36 month compliance gap estimate is plausible/indicative; retains tag appropriately.
